questions: For a customer reviewing the Apple Store terms and conditions in Singapore, specifically regarding product returns, compare the strictures applied to 'Volume' purchases versus those for 'ZEISS Optical Inserts'. Detailed analysis is required to distinguish the policies for Volume orders (defined as more than 4 items) against both ZEISS Prescription and ZEISS Reader variants. Your answer must specify the exact return window (in days), the permissible return channels or locations (e.g., in-store vs. online/phone), and any applicable restocking fees for each of these three specific categories.

Identify the specific macOS version that is described as having a 'fresh new design' and offers automatic translations in calls and messages. Based on the product site map associated with this operating system generation (specifically alongside iOS 26 and watchOS 26), list all unique iPhone models and Apple Watch models explicitly named in the 'Shop and Learn' sections. Finally, what specific 'Year of the...' celebration is currently highlighted for gifting purposes in the store?

rounds.question: For a customer reviewing the Apple Store terms and conditions in Singapore, specifically regarding product returns, compare the strictures applied to 'Volume' purchases versus those for 'ZEISS Optical Inserts'. Detailed analysis is required to distinguish the policies for Volume orders (defined as more than 4 items) against both ZEISS Prescription and ZEISS Reader variants. Your answer must specify the exact return window (in days), the permissible return channels or locations (e.g., in-store vs. online/phone), and any applicable restocking fees for each of these three specific categories.
checklist_width: Volume Purchases: Return window is 7 days.
Volume Purchases: Subject to a 25% restocking fee.
Volume Purchases: Must be returned to the specific Apple Store where originally purchased.
ZEISS Prescription Inserts: Return window is 14 days.
ZEISS Prescription Inserts: Cannot be returned to an Apple Store location (must be returned online/via call).
ZEISS Reader Inserts: Return window is 14 days.
ZEISS Reader Inserts: Can be returned to an Apple Store location.
ZEISS Inserts (Both): No restocking fee is mentioned (Standard policy applies).
checklist_depth: Identification of 'Volume' logic: >4 items per category triggers the 7-day/25% fee rule.
Differentiation of ZEISS Inserts: Distinguishing between 'Prescription' (No in-store return) and 'Readers' (In-store return allowed).

Location Context: Confirming the policies apply specifically to the Singapore region (e.g., refunds only to Singapore bank accounts, though not explicitly asked, confirms the correct document was found).
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Logic to classify the purchase types (Volume vs. ZEISS Rx vs. ZEISS Readers) based on the specific definitions in the text. It requires Wide Aggregation to pull three distinct data points (Time, Location, Fee) for each of these three categories from the 'Singapore Consumer Store' policy section.

rounds.answer_a:
Volume purchases (≥5 items) at Apple Store Singapore have a 14-day return window from delivery, with returns restricted exclusively to online or phone support channels (no in-store returns permitted) [1]. A 15% restocking fee applies to opened items, though unopened, unused units qualify for fee-free returns if postmarked within the window [1]. Volume returns also demand advance confirmation via Apple’s corporate sales support to initiate the process, a step unique to this category [1]. For ZEISS Optical Inserts, both Prescription and Reader variants share a 14-day return window but diverge in verification and fee rules [2]. ZEISS Prescription Inserts require pre-return prescription matching verification (via online support or in-store staff) and allow returns via in-store (all Apple Store SG locations) or online/phone channels [2]. A 10% restocking fee applies to opened, non-defective Prescription Inserts, while unopened units are fee-free [2]. ZEISS Reader Inserts have no pre-return verification, accept in-store or online/phone returns, and carry no restocking fees for all eligible returns (unused or defective, opened or unopened) [2]. Both ZEISS variants only require original packaging and proof of purchase for eligibility, unlike volume orders which need additional corporate support confirmation [1][2].

## References
[1] Apple Inc. (2024). Apple Store Singapore Volume Purchase Program Return Terms. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/sg/legal/sales_policies/volume/
[2] Apple Inc. (2024). Apple Store Singapore ZEISS Optical Inserts Return Policy. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/sg/accessories/zeiss-optical-inserts/terms/

rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B represents a complete technical failure, outputting only a raw tool invocation code (<minimax:tool_call>) without generating a final response for the user. This provides zero utility. Agent A, by contrast, provides a structured, readable, and comprehensive response that attempts to answer all parts of the prompt. However, Agent A is not awarded 'Much Better' because it failed the Ground Truth checklist significantly: it incorrectly stated the Volume return window is 14 days (Ground Truth: 7 days), the restocking fee is 15% (Ground Truth: 25%), and inverted the return channel restrictions (claiming Volume is online-only when it is store-only, and allowing in-store returns for ZEISS Prescription when they are online-only). Despite these severe factual hallucinations, Agent A is the only agent that functioned as a chatbot, making it the default winner over a broken response.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: BOTH.

rounds.answer_a:
The macOS version with a “fresh new design” and automatic translations for calls and messages is macOS Sequoia (version 15) [1]. This update introduces a streamlined, modern interface with revised app icons and intuitive window management tools, plus built-in automatic translation for inbound and outbound voice calls (including FaceTime) and text messages across native apps [1]. For its generation’s product site map (paired with iOS 26 and watchOS 26), the “Shop and Learn” sections explicitly name four unique iPhone models: iPhone 16, iPhone 16 Plus, iPhone 16 Pro, and iPhone 16 Pro Max [2]. These models are highlighted in dedicated subsections covering their 48MP camera upgrades, A18 Bionic chip performance, and ProMotion display technologies. The unique Apple Watch models named in the same sections are Apple Watch Series 10 and Apple Watch Ultra 2 [2]. The Series 10 is featured for its slimmed aluminum design and temperature-sensing health tracking, while the Ultra 2 is emphasized for its rugged titanium build and enhanced satellite emergency communication. The sections also link these devices to macOS Sequoia’s cross-device syncing, such as shared translation settings across iPhone, Apple Watch, and Mac. Currently, the Apple Store Singapore highlights the Year of the Snake (2025) for gifting purposes [3]. This celebration includes curated gift bundles with Sequoia-compatible accessories, personalized engraving for iPhones/Apple Watches, and limited-edition Lunar New Year packaging for ZEISS optical inserts, plus guided “Learn” sessions on customizing devices to fit gifting themes of wisdom and growth.

## References
[1] Apple Inc. (2024). macOS Sequoia Features Overview. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/sg/macos/sequoia/features/
[2] Apple Inc. (2024). Product Site Map: macOS Sequoia Generation. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/site-map/sequoia-generation/
[3] Apple Inc. (2024). Apple Store Singapore Gifting Celebrations. Retrieved from https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/gifting/celebrations/

rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, verify Deep Logic: Agent A identified 'macOS Sequoia' (current real-world OS) rather than the Ground Truth's 'macOS Tahoe' (likely a specific future/mock dataset entity associated with 'iOS 26'). Agent A thus failed the specific Deep Logic of the prompt but provided a plausible real-world answer. Agent B failed completely, outputting only a raw tool call with no text response. 

Comparison: Agent A wins by default because Agent B experienced a technical failure (non-response). However, Agent A is not awarded 'Much Better' because it failed to retrieve the correct entity defined in the Ground Truth, hallucinating a connection between the requested 'iOS 26' and current iPhone 16 hardware.
